mechanism_narrative: A prolonged federal funds rate near zero compresses the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS levels below 0.5% fail to coincide with positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that increases wage costs and compresses profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE readings below 5.0% are followed by negative 60-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and consumer prices generates demand-pull inflation that raises input costs faster than output prices, squeezing corporate profitability and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL over a 3-month window fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a measured increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the risk-free discount rate and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, FEDFUNDS increases exceeding 0.25 percentage points over 3 months are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between housing starts and payroll employment captures the joint health of residential investment and labor markets; lower values of both variables simultaneously reduce aggregate demand expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production reflects rising corporate output and improving operating leverage, which boosts earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% annualized triggers expectations of preemptive Federal Reserve tightening, which increases real discount rates and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL annualized changes above 2.5% are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 55% of triggered months over any 2-year rolling window.
